name: The Cueva del Turche and the Palomas - Circular from Buñol
name_original: La Cueva del Turche y las Palomas — Circular desde Buñol
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Buñol navigate a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous surroundings, river systems, and varied terrain. The region is encircled by the Sierra de Las Cabrillas, Sierra de Dos Aguas, and Sierra de Malacara y Martés mountain ranges, offering challenging climbs and scenic vistas. Riders encounter a mix of rolling hills, pine forests, and technical sections with roots and rocks, shaped by the Buñol and Juanes rivers. This environment provides a network of both paved and unpaved routes suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Buñol The most popular mountain bike trail is The Cueva del Turche and the Palomas - Circular from Buñol, a 30.8 miles (49.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes by notable natural water features and geological formations. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los Molinos Trail – View of Turís loop from Buñol, a moderate 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. This route offers views towards Turís and traverses rolling hills and pine-covered slopes. Local mountain bikers also love the Prado Trail – View of Turís loop from Buñol, a 24.9 miles (40.2 km) trail leading through pine forests and open landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Buñol is defined by the Sierra de Las Cabrillas mountain range, the Buñol River, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Buñol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Buñol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Buñol?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Buñol, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes navigate mountainous surroundings, river systems, and varied terrain, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Buñol?
A: You can expect a diverse landscape featuring rolling hills, challenging climbs, and pine-tree-covered slopes. Trails often include technical sections with roots and rocks, as well as both paved and unpaved surfaces. The region is encircled by the Sierra de Las Cabrillas, Sierra de Dos Aguas, and Sierra de Malacara y Martés mountain ranges, providing scenic vistas and varied terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Buñol?
A: Yes, Buñol offers several trails suitable for beginners. There are 4 easy routes available, providing a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the region. These trails are generally less demanding in terms of elevation and technical difficulty.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Buñol?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Buñol features 20 difficult mountain bike routes. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908564262/the-cueva-del-turche-and-the-palomas-circular-from-bunol" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cueva del Turche and the Palomas - Circular from Buñol&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.8-mile trail with significant elevation changes that passes by notable natural water features and geological formations.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Buñol?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Buñol are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31743137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Molinos Trail – View of Turís loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38265952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prado Trail – View of Turís loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Buñol?
A: Buñol is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning features like the Buñol River, ravines, and waterfalls. The renowned Ruta del Agua (Water Route) includes highlights such as the &lt;strong&gt;Cueva del Turche&lt;/strong&gt; with its impressive cascade and lake, and the &lt;strong&gt;Cueva de las Palomas&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its waterfall and cave system. You might also pass by natural pools like Charco Mañán along the Juanes River. For more details on gorges, you can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de Papan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks accessible by mountain bike in Buñol?
A: Yes, the historic Buñol Castle is a prominent landmark that can be incorporated into mountain bike routes, offering panoramic views of the town and surrounding pine-covered mountains. A route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3962177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buñol Castle – Buñol loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes this attraction. The historic Puente República bridge also crosses one of the area's gorges.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Buñol?
A: The mountain bike trails in Buñol are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging climbs to flowy sections, and the scenic beauty of the region, including views of distant peaks and extensive pine forests. The presence of natural water features like the Cueva del Turche is also a significant draw.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Buñol?
A: Buñol offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While trails are accessible in winter, some areas might be cooler. Summers can be warm, but routes through pine forests or near water features like the Cueva del Turche can offer refreshing breaks.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the surrounding mountain ranges?
A: Many routes in Buñol provide excellent views of the surrounding mountain ranges, including the Sierra de Las Cabrillas, Sierra de Dos Aguas, and Sierra de Malacara y Martés. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31743137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Molinos Trail – View of Turís loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt; offers views towards Turís, while routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17321280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Cruz Pass – Puntal de las Palomas loop from Buñol&lt;/a&gt; traverse higher elevations, providing expansive vistas.
tips: Very nice trail for both walking and cycling. Bordering the Buñol river and with very nice waterfalls along the ideal route to take a break.
The Turche de Buñol Cave is a beautiful corner worth visiting, especially after abundant rains, at which time we can enjoy a waterfall that falls 60 meters high.

Another beautiful place to enjoy all year round. Cave and waterfall with a very nice recreational area and parking very close. Ideal for hiking and also accessible by mtb, although there will be some sectors where you need to push the bike.
Top with very nice views in all directions, especially on days with high visibility, you will be able to see the city of Valencia completely.
